votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions and specification, maintaining the same logic for digit extraction, palindrome checking, and counting even/odd palindromes.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, correctly implementing the digit extraction, palindrome checking, and the specification logic for counting even and odd palindromes.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions and logic, including the digit extraction, palindrome checking, and the specification for counting even and odd palindromes.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. digits_nat extracts digits in the same order, z_to_digits reverses them to get most-significant-first order, is_palindrome checks if digits equal their reverse, seq_Z generates the range [1..n], and even_odd_palindrome_spec correctly counts even and odd palindromes in that range. The logic matches the Coq definitions for all concrete inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: digits are computed with bounded fuel and reversed, is_palindrome checks equality with the reverse, the range is 1..Z.to_nat n, negatives give empty range, and even/odd counts match via parity checks. Concrete outputs align for all inputs.

Python mirrors Coq on all concrete inputs: digit extraction (with fuel), z_to_digits via nonnegative cast, palindrome check by list equality, range [1..Z.to_nat n], and counting evens/odds among palindromes. Negative or zero n yields empty range in both. Equality checks yield the same judgments.

Python mirrors the Coq functions: same digit extraction (with fuel), same palindrome check (empty list for n<=0), same range 1..Z.to_nat n, and same even/odd counting. Concrete behavior matches for all inputs.
